editorial.title: The great australian attention deficit
editorial.teaser: With councils backflipping, airlines grounding planes, and the colonel colonising vietnamese cuisine, it’s clear we’ve all lost the plot—and possibly our scripts.
editorial.body:
It appears the northern beaches council has been taking its policy advice from a tumble dryer. Between reversing decisions on seniors and performing acrobatic backflips on housing rules to entice developers into "failed" suburbs, our local governance is displaying the stability of a chemically imbalanced unicycle.

one moment you’re in limbo, the next you’re being slurred by a lifesaver; it is less a local government area and more a sociological experiment in whiplash. But perhaps we shouldn't judge them too harshly.

according to the deluge of reports coming out of nsw health, we are in the golden age of adhd. Everyone is getting a diagnosis!

gps are handing them out, support groups are forming, and experts are eagerly pointing out symptoms. Suddenly, the council’s erratic behavior makes perfect sense.

why stick to a housing policy when something shiny and developer-shaped wanders past? this national inability to focus isn't isolated to the peninsula.

look at jetstar, who cancelled 90 flights because, presumably, they simply forgot they owned planes. Or look at the culinary abomination that is the kfc bánh mì.

only a nation suffering from a profound collective distraction would allow a fried chicken purveyor to assault a baguette and call it culture. It’s not a sandwich; it’s a cry for help.

even barnaby joyce seems to be benefitting from the chaos, with pundits suggesting a "change of scenery" rather than a change of personality. Perhaps he just needs a fidget spinner?

in the end, the only people displaying any discipline are the children participating in the local triathlon. While the adults scream about offshore bushfires and argue over picture books, the kids are quietly swimming, biking, and running in a straight line.

we should probably put them in charge of the council immediately. At least they can finish a race.
editorial.author: Professor Remy
